label: Suturesill House
description: Suturesill House is a narrow, vertical residence set into Seamline Concord’s fault-limned escarpment, its basalt-glass façade scored with deliberate seam-grooves that catch the copper-white twilight in measured bands. Interior partitions are formed from laminar mirror-slag panels, producing long axial reflections that echo the city’s calibrated sightlines toward the auroral girdle. Electrostatic baffles at the vents pin Lacrymara’s charged glass dust into stable filigree along lintels and stairwells, turning accumulation into ornament rather than grit. A cistern-court at the core holds a shallow sheet of reflective particulates, functioning as both household water reserve and a private optical marker within the hushlit lattice.
